subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content: 21 Character Cards total: 2 Spy (0), 6 Guard (1), 2 Priest (2), 2 Baron (3), 2 Handmaid (4), 2 Prince (5), 2 Chancellor (6), 1 King (7), 1 Countess (8), 1 Princess (9). Also 6 Reference Cards, 13 Favor Tokens, and 1 cloth bag.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content: Give each player 1 reference card (return extras to box). Shuffle all 21 character cards into a facedown deck. Set aside the top card facedown without looking. In a 2-player game, set aside 3 additional cards faceup. Deal 1 starting hand card to each player.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content: First player is the person who most recently hand-wrote a letter. In later rounds, first player is the previous round winner; if tied, randomly choose among tied players. Play proceeds clockwise.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content: On your turn: 1) Draw 1 card from the deck. 2) Choose 1 of your 2 cards and play it faceup in your play area. 3) Resolve that card's effect fully. 4) Keep the other card in hand. 5) Pass turn clockwise. If you are out of the round, your turn is skipped.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content: You always play exactly one card each turn from your two-card hand state. Card effects: Princess (9): if played or discarded for any reason, you are immediately out. Countess (8): no effect, but must be played if your other card is King or Prince (this restriction does not apply during temporary Chancellor draw handling). King (7): choose another player, trade hands. Chancellor (6): draw 2 cards, keep 1 of the 3 cards now in hand, put other 2 on bottom in any order; with only 1 deck card left draw 1 and return 1; with no deck cards, no effect. Prince (5): choose any player (including yourself); that player discards hand without resolving it and draws a new card; if deck empty, draws facedown set-aside card. Handmaid (4): until start of your next turn, other players cannot choose you for card effects. Baron (3): choose another player; secretly compare hands; lower value is out (tie: nobody out). Priest (2): choose another player; secretly look at their hand. Guard (1): choose another player and name a character other than Guard; if named card matches their hand, they are out. Spy (0): no immediate effect. If all other in-round players are protected by Handmaid and your card requires choosing another player (Guard/Priest/Baron/King), card resolves with no effect. If your card requires choosing any player (Prince), you must choose yourself in that case.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content: Elimination handling: when you are knocked out, discard your hand faceup without resolving that card's effect, flip reference card to broken seal side, you cannot be chosen for effects, and your turns are skipped until next round. Open information rule: all played and discarded cards remain visible to everyone. Spy bonus rule: at round end, if you are the only player still in the round who played or discarded a Spy, gain 1 extra favor token (this is separate from round-win token and maxes at 1 token even if you played/discarded 2 Spies).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content: A round ends either when the deck runs out or when only one player remains in the round. If deck runs out, all remaining players reveal hand cards; highest value wins the round and gains 1 favor token. If tied for highest value, all tied players each gain 1 favor token. If only one player remains, that player immediately wins the round and gains 1 favor token. Additional token source: Spy bonus token as specified by Spy rule. Game ends when at least one player reaches the required favor tokens by player count: 2 players = 6 tokens, 3 players = 5, 4 players = 4, 5 players = 3, 6 players = 3. Multiple players can win simultaneously. No further tiebreaker is applied beyond simultaneous win allowance.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content: Track every faceup discard from turn one; deduction improves Guard calls and Prince targets immediately. Use early Priest/Baron safely to narrow possibilities before making high-risk guesses. Preserve flexibility by not exposing your hand value pattern unless it gains concrete advantage. If you hold Handmaid, time it before likely attack windows rather than just playing it on cooldown.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content: 1) Random Guard naming with no evidence, which wastes the strongest repeat card. 2) Forgetting mandatory Countess play when holding King or Prince. 3) Revealing too much table talk after seeing a hand with Priest, letting opponents infer hidden information. 4) Mis-timing Handmaid so it protects you when nobody can threaten you anyway. 5) Ignoring the Spy bonus and accidentally giving up a free favor token race edge.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content: 1) Information vs aggression: do you use Priest/Baron to confirm reads first, or fire Guard now before cards rotate? 2) Survival vs pressure: play Handmaid defensively, or push elimination tempo with Prince/Guard and accept exposure. 3) End-round value management: if deck is thinning, decide whether to protect a high card for showdown or force outs before comparison.

metadata.notes: Primary source: Love Letter rules text provided in prompt (Z-Man Games edition, 2019).
